title: Node 24, TypeScript 7.0.2, TypeScript 6 API Compatibility, npm, and Reproducible Installs · Run and Artifact Forensics
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective reproducible TypeScript build contract; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in node 24, typescript 7.0.2, typescript 6 api compatibility, npm, and reproducible installs, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible TypeScript build contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Align Node 24, npm 11 or later, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API required by current tooling, package metadata, engines, action runtime, and local developer versions.
Use npm ci with a committed matching lockfile and committed install flags while detecting lock drift and registry changes.
Bound dependency lifecycle scripts, registry authentication, private package access, network egress, install auditing, and secret exposure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment delivery-system evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, keep Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-reproducibility-changed-environment-uq0v78". Tailor its typescript-build.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-reproducibility-changed-environment-uq0v78
  - competency: ci-reproducibility-changed-environment
    artifact: typescript-build.yaml
    decision: reproducibility changed environment
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: rotate a cloud trust condition during deployment
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: uq0v78
    change: |
      node: 24-lts
      npm: 11-or-later
      typescript-native: 7.0.2
      typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
      tsc: native-compiler
      api-tools: typescript6-alias
      setup-node: 7
      install: npm-ci-frozen-lock
      package-manager-cache: risk-reviewed
      workspace: clean-checkout
      build-output: content-digested
      changed-environment: cold-cache-and-locale
    verify: npm ci then run strict type test build and package checks from a clean supported Node 24 environment
    failure: Reject floating toolchains, npm install lock mutation, local residue, lifecycle-script trust, or one warm build as reproducibility.
    repair: Align tool versions, freeze installs, bound registry and scripts, build cleanly, digest output, and repeat changed environments.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Matching application tests prove byte-identical or behaviorally equivalent builds.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, keep Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Matching application tests prove byte-identical or behaviorally equivalent builds.
